description_long: This object is a single-bladed axe featuring a broad, curved steel blade with a pronounced beard and a slightly pointed poll. The blade is securely mounted to a long, gently curved wooden handle, which appears to be made of a dense hardwood and is finished in a natural brown color. The handle is smooth, with no visible grip enhancements, and tapers slightly toward the end for ergonomic handling. The blade has a matte, slightly rough finish, suggesting it is forged or cast rather than highly polished. The mounting of the blade to the handle is traditional, with a visible wedge securing the head. There are no visible markings, engravings, or labels on either the blade or the handle.
description: A traditional, single-bladed axe with a curved wooden handle and a broad, matte steel blade. The axe has a natural wood finish and a robust, functional design.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the top, showing the narrow profile of the blade and the rounded end of the handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the side, clearly displaying the curved wooden handle and the broad, bearded steel blade.
description_view_2: The axe is seen from the handle end, highlighting the long, straight shaft and the minimal width of the blade from this angle.
description_view_3: The opposite side view of the axe, again showing the full blade profile and the curvature of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle, smooth finish, oval cross-section, no added grip features, natural wood grain visible.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad steel blade with a pronounced beard, curved cutting edge, and a slightly pointed poll; no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade head fitted onto the handle and secured by a visible wedge.
